zh.lifeSummary: Sir William Gordon 1st Of Lochinvar於1425年出生在蘇格蘭艾斯爾吉爾馬爾諾可。他和Mariota Janet Maxwell Of Calderwood至少有2個兒子和1個女兒。他於1466年被列為he held the office of great chamberlain [scotland] between 1466 and 1469。他去世於1460年4月1日在英國諾圖波藍德阿恩維克享年35歲，埋葬在英國蘇格蘭易亞斯特 艾斯爾吉爾馬爾諾可。

nameMeanings.name: Gordon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from Gordon in Berwickshire, named with Welsh gor ‘spacious’ + din ‘fort’.
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Gourdon in Saône-et-Loire, so called from the Gallo-Roman personal name Gordus + the locative suffix -o, -ōnis.
English (of Norman origin): alternatively, said to be a nickname from a diminutive of Old French gourd ‘heavy, dull, sluggish’ (compare 8 below).

variants.name: Gordin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Eyal, Moisey, Yakov, Zalman, Erez, Faina, Haya, Mendel.  Russian Leonid, Aleksandr, Boris, Feiga, Genady.
French: variant of   Gourdin  . This surname is very rare in France.

variants.name: Gordan
meanings: German: variant of Jordan . The surname Gordan is very rare in Germany.
Irish (Wexford and Sligo): adopted as an Anglicized form of Mag Mhuirneacháin ‘son of Muirneachán’, a personal name from a diminutive of muirneach ‘lovable’.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: Sir William Gordon 1st Of Lochinvar was born in 1425, in Kilmarnock, Ayrshire, Scotland. He had at least 2 sons and 1 daughter with Mariota Janet Maxwell Of Calderwood. In 1466, his occupation is listed as he held the office of great chamberlain [scotland] between 1466 and 1469. He died on 1 April 1460, in Alnwick, Northumberland, England, at the age of 35, and was buried in Kilmarnock, East Ayrshire, Scotland, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Sir William Gordon 1st Of Lochinvar est né en 1425, à Kilmarnock, Ayrshire, Écosse. Il a eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille avec Mariota Janet Maxwell Of Calderwood. En 1466, sa profession de son indiquée est he held the office of great chamberlain [scotland] between 1466 and 1469. Il est décédé le 1 avril 1460, à Alnwick, Northumberland, Angleterre, à l’âge de 35 ans, et a été enterré à Kilmarnock, East Ayrshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni.

de.Kilmarnock, East Ayrshire, Scotland: Kilmarnock, East Ayrshire, Schottland, United Kingdom
de.lifeSummary: Sir William Gordon 1st Of Lochinvar wurde 1425 in Kilmarnock, Ayrshire, Scotland geboren. Er hatte mit Mariota Janet Maxwell Of Calderwood mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter. 1466 ist sein Beruf als he held the office of great chamberlain [scotland] between 1466 and 1469 aufgeführt. Er starb am 1. April 1460 im Alter von 35 Jahren in Alnwick, Northumberland, England und wurde in Kilmarnock, East Ayrshire, Schottland, United Kingdom begraben.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir William Gordon 1st Of Lochinvarは1425年にスコットランドエイルシュアイアキルマルノックで生まれた。彼はMariota Janet Maxwell Of Calderwoodとの間に少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘をもうけた。1466年，職業はhe held the office of great chamberlain [scotland] between 1466 and 1469と記録されている。彼は1460年4月1日にイギリスノースウンバーランドアルンウィックで35歳で死去し，イギリススコットランドエアスト エイルシュアイアキルマルノックにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir William Gordon 1st Of Lochinvar(은)는 1425년 스컷랜드 에이르실르 킬마노c크에서 태어났다. 그는 Mariota Janet Maxwell Of Calderwood(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 1466년, 그의 직업은 he held the office of great chamberlain [scotland] between 1466 and 1469이었다. 그는 1460년 4월 1일 영국 노르스벌랜드 알윅에서 35세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 스코틀랜드 이스트 에이르실르 킬마노c크에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Sir William Gordon 1st Of Lochinvar nasceu em 1425, em Kilmarnock, Ayrshire, Scotland. Ele teve pelo menos 2 filhos e 1 filha com Mariota Janet Maxwell Of Calderwood. Em 1466, sua profissão aparece como he held the office of great chamberlain [scotland] between 1466 and 1469. Ele faleceu em 1 de abril de 1460, em Alnwick, Northumberland, Inglaterra, com 35 anos, e foi sepultado em Kilmarnock, East Ayrshire, Escócia, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Sir William Gordon 1st Of Lochinvar родился в 1425 г. в местности килмарнокк, аиршир, Шотландия. У него было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь от Mariota Janet Maxwell Of Calderwood. В 1466 году он работал по профессии he held the office of great chamberlain [scotland] between 1466 and 1469. Он умер 1 апреля 1460 г. в местности алнвикк, нортумбирланд, Англия в возрасте 35 лет и был похоронен в местности килмарнокк, эаст аиршир, Шотландия, Великобритания.
